Hearing On Defendant'S Request For Discretionary Stay (Per 8/12/04 Ex Parte Order)
Off Calendar Aug-17-2004 Continued To Aug-23-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sherri Sokeland Kaiser, Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-4691 for Respondent and Moving Party City and County of San Francisco and Martin
Mara E. Rosales, Esq., Meyers Nave Riback Silver & Wilson (510) 808-2108 for Respondent and Moving Party City and County of San Francisco and Martin
Sharon L. Browne, Esq., Pacific Legal Foundation (916) 419-7111 for Petitioner and Opposing Party Coral Construction
RULING:
A R G U E D. The Court finds that its recent order re constitutionality of the City's MBE/WBE programs is a prohibiting injunction and not a mandatory one. Accordingly, the Court's order is not automatically stayed.

The Court agrees to consider a discretionary injunction and orders Counsel to develop a draft form of such order addressing:

1) Contracts already awarded by the City but not finalized; 2) contracts in the "pipe line"; and, 3) contracts already awarded and paid for.

Counsel shall submit their draft order no later than noon on August 20, 2004. Respondent's Request For Discretionary Stay is CONTINUED to august 23, 2004 in Dept. 301.

Effective July 26, 2004, the date of this Court's order finding the City's MBE/WBE program unconstitutional, the City is prohibited from entering into contracts that violate Prop. 209 other than as discussed above. No discretionary injunction will be entered at this time.

Counsel may submit additional briefing to address additional issues of conflict on shortened time. (301/JLW/DS) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
